PLEASE REPLY TO-
California pair PoliticaI
Practices Commission
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA 95804-0807
Attention Wayne Imberi, Consultant
R€: CR&R INCORPORATED
Dear Mr. Imberi:
At the suggestion of Gloria Perez with the Politlcal 
Reform
Divisionr w€ are writing you in an attempt to clear up confusion
as to who the lobbyist firm of z. Harry Astor represents. 
In
addition to my representation of the California Refuse Removal
Councilr on behalf of CR&R Incorporated we filed a Form 605,
Lobbying Firm Registration dated June l, 1990, a Form 602,
Lobbying Firm Authorization dated May 18, 1990 and a Form 601,
Lobbying Firm Registration bearing a date of June L, 1990.
On February 7, L992r w€ received a letter from Bob Steele of the
Political Reform Division indicating they had received a Lobbying
Firm Activity Authorization (Form 602) from CR&R Inc. and Mobile
Recycling Corp. dated January 27, 1992 authorizi-ng our firm to
lobby on their behalf, however they did not have from us the
documents necessary to add Mobile Recycling Corp. to our
registration. 
Prior to January L, L992 this office did not lobby
on behalf of Mobile Recycling, Inc. We then completed an
Amendment to Registration (Form 605) and Lobbying Firm
Registration Statement (Form 60l-Part II only) effective January
1992 reflecting our representation of CR&R Incorporated and its
wholly owned subsidiary, Mobile Recycling Corp. (which is the
accurate relationship of the two entities ). At t.his point we had
hoped to clarify registrat.ion of this client.

what, and from whom, do you need information and/or documents to

If you should have additional questions after reviewing the
contents of this letter, please contact my secretary Nancy Lutz
for further assistance.

sections.question_synthetic: The sender seeks clarification from FPPC regarding proper registration and documentation requirements for a lobbying firm representing CR&R Incorporated and its subsidiary Mobile Recycling Corp., and requests guidance on what additional documents or information FPPC needs to complete the registration process.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is an incoming request/correspondence letter from a law firm to FPPC, not an FPPC advice letter response. No traditional QUESTION or CONCLUSION sections present. Document discusses lobbying firm registration forms (601, 602, 605) and seeks guidance from FPPC on final documentation requirements.

embedding.qa_text: QUESTION: The sender seeks clarification from FPPC regarding proper registration and documentation requirements for a lobbying firm representing CR&R Incorporated and its subsidiary Mobile Recycling Corp., and requests guidance on what additional documents or information FPPC needs to complete the registration process.

On February 7, L992r w€ received a letter from Bob Steele of the Political Reform Division indicating they had received a Lobbying Firm Activity Authorization (Form 602) from CR&R Inc. and Mobile Recycling Corp. dated January 27, 1992 authorizi-ng our firm to lobby on their behalf, however they did not have from us the documents necessary to add Mobile Recycling Corp. to our registration. embedding.summary: A law firm (Z. Harry Astor) writes to FPPC requesting clarification on lobbying firm registration requirements after receiving guidance from the Political Reform Division. The firm had registered to represent CR&R Incorporated and subsequently needed to add Mobile Recycling Corp. (a wholly owned subsidiary) to their registration with appropriate amended forms.
